---
title: "Reveal.js A11y"
subtitle: "Accessibility Plugin Demo"
authors:
  - name: "Mickaël CANOUIL, _Ph.D._"
    orcid: "0000-0002-3396-4549"
    url: "https://mickael.canouil.fr"
format:
  revealjs:
    output-file: index
    revealjs-plugins:
      - a11y
    revealjs-a11y:
      skip-navigation: true
      focus-indicators: true
      reduced-motion: true
      high-contrast: false
      font-size-controls: true
      font-selection: true
      text-spacing: true
      link-highlight: true
      slide-landmarks: true
      alt-text-warnings: true
      announce-slide-numbers: true
      announce-fragments: true
      announce-language-changes: true
      transcript: true
      pointer-indicator: true
      slide-menu-a11y: true
      slide-change-cue:
        visual: true
        audio: true
      menu: true
---

## Introduction {.smaller}

The **Reveal.js A11y** plugin enhances *accessibility* for keyboard users, screen readers, and users with [visual preferences]{.underline}.

It follows the [Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/) to make presentations usable by everyone.

This deck demonstrates every feature of the plugin alongside a broad range of Quarto formatting and layout options.

::: {.notes}
Welcome to the accessibility plugin demo.
This presentation is designed to exercise as many Quarto Reveal.js features as possible, so the plugin can be tested against real-world content types.
:::

## How to Use This Deck

{{< kbd Tab >}}
: Skip navigation and focus cycling.

{{< kbd "A" mode=plain >}}
: Open or close the accessibility settings menu.

{{< kbd "+" mode=plain >}} / {{< kbd "-" mode=plain >}} / {{< kbd "0" mode=plain >}}
: Increase, decrease, or reset the font size.

{{< kbd Right >}} / {{< kbd Left >}}
: Navigate between slides.

## Skip Navigation {auto-animate=true}

1. [Press {{< kbd Tab >}} when the presentation loads.]{.fragment .fade-in}
2. [A "Skip to content" link appears at the top of the page.]{.fragment .fade-in}
3. [Press {{< kbd Enter >}} to jump past navigation to the slide content.]{.fragment .fade-in}

## Skip Navigation {auto-animate=true}

> A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple Web pages.

--- WCAG 2.4.1, Bypass Blocks^[See <https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#bypass-blocks> for the full success criterion.]

## Focus Indicators & Link Highlight {.smaller}

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}

### Focus indicators

Interactive elements receive enhanced focus outlines when navigating with the keyboard.

Try tabbing through these links:

- [WCAG overview](https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/).
- [WAI-ARIA practices](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/).

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

### Link highlight

All links are [underlined by default]{style="text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 2px;"} so they are distinguishable without relying on colour alone.

This meets WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Colour).

:::

:::

## Reduced Motion & Slide Change Cues {.smaller}

::: {.callout-note}
## Reduced motion

The plugin respects the `prefers-reduced-motion` media query.
When enabled in your operating system settings, all slide transitions are disabled automatically.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Slide change cues

A visual sweep appears along the top edge each time you navigate to a new slide.
An optional audio tone can also be played via `slide-change-cue: {visual: true, audio: true}`.
:::


## High Contrast Mode

Toggle high contrast mode from the accessibility settings menu.

This switches the presentation to a high contrast colour scheme:

- [Black text on white background.]{.fragment .fade-in}
- [Underlined links with standard colours.]{.fragment .grow}
- [Bordered code blocks for clarity.]{.fragment .highlight-red}
- [All decorative elements simplified.]{.fragment .semi-fade-out}

::: {.notes}
The `announce-fragments` feature ensures each of these list items is announced to screen readers as they appear.
Try navigating with a screen reader enabled to hear the announcements.
:::

## Font Size Controls {.smaller}

Adjust the font size using keyboard shortcuts.
The setting persists across sessions via `localStorage`.

: Font size control shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action | Range |
|----------|--------|-------|
| {{< kbd "+" mode=plain >}} | Increase font size | Up to $200\%$ |
| {{< kbd "-" mode=plain >}} | Decrease font size | Down to $50\%$ |
| {{< kbd "0" mode=plain >}} | Reset to default | $100\%$ |

The step size is $10\%$ by default and can be configured with `font-size-step`.

## Font Selection

::: {.panel-tabset}

### System Fonts

- System Sans (the operating system default sans-serif).
- System Serif (the operating system default serif).
- System Mono (the operating system default monospace).

### Accessible Fonts

- **Atkinson Hyperlegible**: designed for high legibility by the Braille Institute.
- **Lexend**: reduces visual stress and improves reading performance.
- **OpenDyslexic**: weighted bottoms help prevent letter rotation for dyslexic readers.

### Local Font Picker

On Chromium-based browsers, a system font picker is also available from the settings menu.

This uses the Local Font Access API to list all fonts installed on the user's device.

:::

## Text Spacing {.smaller}

Adjust text spacing from the accessibility settings menu.
All settings persist across sessions.

: Text spacing levels

| Property | Level 0 | Level 1 | Level 2 | Level 3 | Level 4 |
|----------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| Line height | Default | +1 step | +2 steps | +3 steps | +4 steps |
| Letter spacing | Default | +1 step | +2 steps | +3 steps | +4 steps |
| Word spacing | Default | +1 step | +2 steps | +3 steps | +4 steps |

The increment formula is $s_n = s_0 + n \cdot \Delta$ where $s_0$ is the default value, $n$ is the level, and $\Delta$ is the step size.

## Slide Landmarks & Screen Reader Announcements {.smaller}

Each slide is annotated with ARIA landmark roles so screen readers can navigate between them.

```{.yaml}
revealjs-a11y:
  slide-landmarks: true # <1>
  announce-slide-numbers: true # <2>
  announce-fragments: true # <3>
```

1. Adds `role="region"` and `aria-label` to each slide.
2. Announces slide number and title on navigation.
3. Announces fragment content as it appears.

The current slide is marked with `aria-current="step"`.
{.aside}

## Language Change Announcements

When navigating between slides with different `lang` attributes, screen readers are notified of the language change.

Set per-slide language using Quarto's attribute syntax:

```{.markdown}
## Slide Title {lang="fr"}
```

The plugin reads the `lang` attribute from each slide's `<section>` element and announces changes via an ARIA live region.

::: {.notes}
This feature uses `Intl.DisplayNames` to resolve human-readable language names.
Navigate to the next slide to hear a language change announcement.
:::

## Diapositive en français {lang="fr"}

Cette diapositive est en français.
Le lecteur d'écran devrait annoncer le changement de langue.

::: {.notes}
This slide demonstrates language change announcements.
The `{lang="fr"}` attribute on the heading tells Quarto to set `lang="fr"` on the `<section>`.
The plugin detects the change and announces it to screen readers.
:::

## Transcript View

Press {{< kbd "T" mode=plain >}} to open a linearised transcript of the presentation.

The transcript uses explicit `::: {.transcript}` blocks when provided, otherwise it falls back to the slide's visible content.
Speaker notes are never included.

::: {.transcript}
This is an explicitly authored transcript for the transcript view slide.
It demonstrates how authors can provide custom transcript text that is more descriptive than the slide's visual content.
:::

::: {.notes}
The transcript view is useful for screen reader users and anyone who prefers a linear reading experience.
Press T to open it, Escape to close.
:::

## Pointer / Focus Indicator

Press {{< kbd "P" mode=plain >}} to toggle a large, high-visibility spotlight that follows the cursor or keyboard focus.

- Useful for low-vision users following along on a projected display.
- Configurable size and colour via the accessibility settings menu.
- Uses `pointer-events: none` so clicks pass through the spotlight.

::: {.notes}
The pointer indicator tracks the mouse cursor via `mousemove` and keyboard focus via `focusin`.
It uses `requestAnimationFrame` for smooth updates and `will-change: transform` for GPU compositing.
:::

## Alt Text Warnings {.smaller}

Images without alt text are highlighted with a red dashed outline during authoring.

::: {layout-ncol=2}

![A placeholder image with alt text](https://placehold.co/400x200){fig-alt="Placeholder image demonstrating proper alt text usage" width="100%"}

![](https://placehold.co/400x200){width="100%"}

:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Missing alt text

The second image above should display a warning border because it lacks alt text.
Always provide descriptive alt text for meaningful images.
:::

## Accessibility Settings Menu

Press {{< kbd "A" mode=plain >}} to open or close the settings menu.

- **Display**
  - Toggle switches for *high contrast* and *link highlighting*.
  - *Colour overlay* for reading comfort (none, yellow, blue, pink, green).
- **Typography**
  - Sliders for `font-size`, `line-height`, `letter-spacing`, `word-spacing`.
  - Font family selector.
- **Cues**
  - Visual sweep toggle.
  - Audio tone toggle.
- **Reset** all preferences to defaults.

## Configuration Reference {.smaller .scrollable}

```{.yaml code-line-numbers="3-5,8-9"}
format:
  revealjs:
    revealjs-plugins:
      - a11y
    revealjs-a11y:
      skip-navigation: true
      focus-indicators: true
      reduced-motion: true
      high-contrast: false
      font-size-controls: true
      font-selection: true
      text-spacing: true
      link-highlight: true
      slide-landmarks: true
      alt-text-warnings: false
      announce-slide-numbers: true
      slide-change-cue:
        visual: true
        audio: true
      menu: true
```

Access the plugin API programmatically:

```{.javascript}
const a11y = Reveal.getPlugin("RevealjsA11y");
a11y.resetPreferences();
```

## Why Accessibility Matters {background-color="#2a2a2a"}

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.r-fit-text}
$1.3$ billion
:::

people worldwide live with some form of disability.

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

![Accessibility matters](https://placehold.co/400x300){fig-alt="A placeholder representing the universal accessibility symbol."}

:::

:::

## WCAG at a Glance

The four principles of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-alt: "Flowchart showing the four WCAG principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, and Robust."
flowchart LR
  A[Perceivable] --> B[Operable]
  B --> C[Understandable]
  C --> D[Robust]
```

::: {.notes}
- **Perceivable**: information must be presentable in ways users can perceive.
- **Operable**: interface components must be operable by all users.
- **Understandable**: information and operation must be understandable.
- **Robust**: content must be robust enough for diverse user agents and assistive technologies.
:::

## Common Barriers in Presentations {.smaller}

::: {.callout-caution}
## What to avoid

- ~~Relying on colour alone to convey meaning~~.
- Missing alt text on images^[WCAG 1.1.1 requires text alternatives for all non-text content.].
- Low contrast text (below a ratio of 4.5:1).
- Inaccessible form controls, like H~2~O formulas without proper markup.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## What to do instead

- Use text labels, patterns, or icons alongside colour.
- Provide descriptive alt text for every meaningful image.
- Ensure sufficient colour contrast.
- Use semantic HTML and ARIA attributes.
:::

## Building Accessible Slides {auto-animate=true}

```{.markdown code-line-numbers="2"}
![A descriptive caption](image.png){
  fig-alt="Detailed description for screen readers."
}
```

## Building Accessible Slides {auto-animate=true}

```{.markdown code-line-numbers="2-3"}
![A descriptive caption](image.png){
  fig-alt="Detailed description for screen readers."
  width="80%"
}
```

. . .

Every image deserves a description.

## Colour and Contrast

::: {.r-stack}

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out}
::: {style="background: #1a1a1a; color: #ffffff; padding: 2em; border-radius: 0.5em; width: 80%; text-align: center;"}
**Good contrast**: white text on dark background (ratio 15.3:1).
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out}
::: {style="background: #ffffff; color: #767676; padding: 2em; border-radius: 0.5em; width: 80%; text-align: center;"}
**Minimum contrast**: grey text on white background (ratio 4.5:1).
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
::: {style="background: #ffffff; color: #d4d4d4; padding: 2em; border-radius: 0.5em; width: 80%; text-align: center;"}
**Poor contrast**: light grey on white (ratio 1.6:1). Avoid this.
:::
:::

:::

## Semantic Structure {.smaller}

Use semantic [HTML]{.smallcaps} elements to provide structure that assistive technologies can interpret.

```{.html}
<section role="region" aria-label="Introduction"> <!-- <1> -->
  <h2>Welcome</h2>
  <nav aria-label="Slide navigation"> <!-- <2> -->
    <a href="#next">Next slide</a>
  </nav>
  <main> <!-- <3> -->
    <p>Slide content goes here.</p>
  </main>
</section>
```

1. Each slide is a region landmark with an accessible name.
2. Navigation elements are marked with `nav` and labelled.
3. Main content is wrapped in a `main` element.

## Keyboard Navigation Patterns {.smaller}

See also [How to Use This Deck](#how-to-use-this-deck) for plugin-specific shortcuts.

: Standard Reveal.js keyboard interactions

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| {{< kbd Right >}} / {{< kbd Left >}} | Next / previous slide. |
| {{< kbd Up >}} / {{< kbd Down >}} | Next / previous vertical slide. |
| {{< kbd "F" mode=plain >}} | Toggle fullscreen. |
| {{< kbd "S" mode=plain >}} | Open speaker notes view. |
| {{< kbd Escape >}} | Toggle overview mode. |
| {{< kbd "." mode=plain >}} | Pause the presentation. |

## Incremental Content {.center}

::: {.incremental}

- Incremental lists reveal items one at a time.
- This is distinct from fragments, which can apply to any element.
- Each item is announced to screen readers as it appears.

:::

::: {.nonincremental}

- This list appears all at once.
- It uses the `.nonincremental` class to override.

:::

## Overlaying Fragments (PDF Separate) {data-pdf-separate="true"}

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out}
**First view** -- this content replaces the next.
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out}
**Second view** -- each fragment gets its own page in print-pdf mode.
:::

::: {.notes}
This slide uses `{pdf-separate}` to generate separate pages per fragment
state in print-pdf mode, useful for overlaying content like
`fade-in-then-out` fragments.
:::

## Testing with Screen Readers {.smaller}

1. **Enable a screen reader** on your platform:
   - macOS: VoiceOver ({{< kbd "Cmd+F5" mode=plain >}}).
   - Windows: [NVDA](https://www.nvaccess.org/) (free) or Narrator.
   - Linux: [Orca](https://help.gnome.org/users/orca/stable/).
2. **Navigate the presentation** using {{< kbd Tab >}} and arrow keys.
3. **Verify announcements**:
   - Slide numbers and titles are read aloud.
   - Fragment content is announced as it appears.
   - Landmark navigation works between slides.
4. **Check the settings menu**:
   - All controls must be operable via keyboard alone.

## Code Accessibility {.smaller}

Syntax highlighting helps sighted users read code, but screen readers need semantic markup too.

```{.python}
def check_contrast(foreground: str, background: str) -> bool:
    """Check whether two colours meet WCAG AA contrast requirements."""
    ratio = calculate_contrast_ratio(foreground, background)
    return ratio >= 4.5
```

```{.r}
check_contrast <- function(foreground, background) {
  ratio <- calculate_contrast_ratio(foreground, background)
  ratio >= 4.5
}
```

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="2"}
function addAriaLabel(element, label) {
  element.setAttribute("aria-label", label);
}
```

## Mathematical Content in Slides {.smaller}

Mathematical notation can pose accessibility challenges.
Inline example: the contrast ratio is defined as $\frac{L_1 + 0.05}{L_2 + 0.05}$ where $L_1 \geq L_2$.

The WCAG relative luminance formula:

$$L = 0.2126 \cdot R_{\text{sRGB}} + 0.7152 \cdot G_{\text{sRGB}} + 0.0722 \cdot B_{\text{sRGB}}$$

MathJax and KaTeX generate accessible MathML, which screen readers can interpret.
{.aside}

## Background Images {background-image="https://placehold.co/1920x1080" background-opacity="0.15"}

Background images should be used with care.

- Ensure sufficient contrast between text and the background.
- Use `background-opacity` to reduce visual noise.
- Background images are decorative and do not receive alt text.

When the `reduced-motion` feature is active, parallax backgrounds are also disabled.

## Fitting Text to Slides

::: {.r-fit-text}
Accessibility is not optional.
:::

![A placeholder image stretched to fill remaining space](https://placehold.co/800x200){.r-stretch fig-alt="A wide placeholder image demonstrating the r-stretch class."}

## Per-Slide Transitions {transition="zoom"}

This slide uses a `zoom` transition instead of the default.

When the `reduced-motion` feature is active, this transition is automatically disabled and replaced with an instant change.

The visual sweep cue still fires to confirm the slide has changed.

## Summary of A11y Features {.smaller .scrollable}

: Plugin feature reference

| Feature | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| `skip-navigation` | `true` | Adds a skip-to-content link for keyboard users. |
| `focus-indicators` | `true` | Enhanced 3px blue outlines on interactive elements. |
| `reduced-motion` | `true` | Disables transitions when OS prefers reduced motion. |
| `high-contrast` | `false` | Black text on white, bordered code blocks. |
| `font-size-controls` | `true` | Keyboard shortcuts (+, -, 0) for font size. |
| `font-selection` | `true` | Font family chooser in the settings menu. |
| `text-spacing` | `true` | Line height, letter spacing, word spacing controls. |
| `link-highlight` | `true` | Underlines all links by default (WCAG 1.4.1). |
| `slide-landmarks` | `true` | ARIA landmark roles on each slide. |
| `alt-text-warnings` | `false` | Red dashed outline on images missing alt text. |
| `announce-slide-numbers` | `true` | Screen reader announces slide number and title. |
| `announce-fragments` | `true` | Screen reader announces fragment content. |
| `slide-menu-a11y` | `true` | Patches reveal.js-menu for screen readers. |
| `slide-change-cue` | `true` | Visual sweep and optional audio tone on navigation. |
| `menu` | `true` | Accessibility settings menu (press A). |

## Resources & Further Reading

- [WCAG 2.2 specification](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/)^[The current stable version of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines.].
- [WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/).
- [WebAIM Contrast Checker](https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/).
- [Inclusive Design Principles](https://inclusivedesignprinciples.info/).
- [Reveal.js A11y Plugin](https://github.com/mcanouil/quarto-revealjs-a11y)^[The source repository for this accessibility plugin.].

## Thank You {background-color="#2a2a2a"}

**Reveal.js A11y Plugin**

---

Learn more and contribute at the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/mcanouil/quarto-revealjs-a11y).

Accessibility benefits everyone.

## Credits {visibility="uncounted"}

This plugin was created by [Mickaël Canouil](https://mickael.canouil.fr/).

It is released under the [MIT Licence](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

Special thanks to the Quarto and Reveal.js communities for their work on accessible web technologies.
